goal: Land the two G3 (Knowledge) external-binary plugin manifests for Phase 3 Week 9 — `plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml` (P3-W9-F05) and `plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml` (P3-W9-F06) — plus their devtools install hints, an end-to-end `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs` integration test exercising real `PluginManager::health_check` round-trips, and two acceptance scripts at `scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh` and `scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh` that pair an MCP `tools/list` smoke with a feature-specific tool-level smoke (F05: symbol-lookup result-shape; F06: store/retrieve/list memory CRUD result-shape). The pattern is the verbatim WO-0044 ast-grep+probe template applied to G3, so no daemon source-code changes are required. Cite master-plan §3.1 line 311-312 (Codebase-Memory + Mem0 P0 listings), §5.3 lines 463-475 (G3 sources), §10 line 963 (codebase-memory-mcp as a stdio MCP server), §14.1 (manifest categories), §18 Phase 3 Week 9 line 1804 ('G3 (Knowledge): Install Codebase-Memory MCP + Mem0') verbatim in the rustdoc + commit body. Cite WO-0044 (precedent), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), and the WO-0068 lessons-learned `scope_out` carve-out for verification-reports/effectiveness-*.md gate-side artefact commits.
plan_summary: Three groups of artefacts with zero overlap to existing daemon source: (A) Two new plugin manifest TOMLs at `plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml` and `plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml`. Both follow the existing `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml` shape: leading TOML comment block citing upstream URL + pinned version + revisit clause; `[plugin]` (name, version, description); `[capabilities]` (provides + languages); `[transport]` (stdio with a pinned canonical MCP-server launch command — executor researches the canonical npm/uv invocation; pin to a specific tag/commit per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` reproducibility); `[lifecycle]` (hot_cold = true; idle_timeout_minutes = 10). (B) One new integration test file `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs` (separate from `plugin_manifests.rs` to keep WO-0044 regression guard untouched) wrapped in `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` with two `#[tokio::test]` async tests — `codebase_memory_manifest_health_check` and `mem0_manifest_health_check` — each loads the on-disk manifest, calls `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(..., FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS)`, and asserts `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok` + `!health.tools.is_empty()` + at least one expected tool name (executor pins canonical names from upstream's `tools/list`). (C) Two acceptance scripts `scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh` and `scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh` rewriting the current stubs end-to-end: (i) detect upstream binary on PATH (operator-actionable hint citing the install scripts); (ii) print version; (iii) run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests <test_name>` and assert the alternation regex `'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'`; (iv) run a feature-specific tool-level smoke (F05: invoke the plugin's symbol-lookup tool against `tests/fixtures/rust-project` and assert the response is JSON with at least one expected field; F06: invoke Mem0's store/retrieve/list tools in sequence against an ephemeral tmpdir-rooted store and assert the round-trip preserves the observation; both gated by `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E=1` env opt-out for air-gapped CI but the verifier MUST NOT set the skip env). (D) Two devtools install scripts `scripts/devtools/install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh` and `scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh` documenting and reproducing the binary install (no auto-execute side effects). NO changes to daemon source code, NO new daemon Cargo deps, NO touching `plugins/structural/serena/`, `plugins/structural/ast-grep/`, `plugins/structural/scip/`, `plugins/search/probe/`, `plugins/search/ripgrep/`. The deferred re-exports of `classify_query` + `parse_reason` from WO-0067 / WO-0068 lessons remain deferred — F05/F06 are plugin-manifest-only WOs and do NOT touch the classify-then-dispatch wiring; that re-export bundle goes to the FIRST daemon-side WO that wires the classifier into orchestration (a future WO that touches `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` or `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs`).
scope_in: 1. plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml — NEW. Same structural shape as `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml`. Leading TOML comment block (~14-25 lines) citing: (a) upstream URL — executor researches the canonical codebase-memory-mcp upstream (the master-plan §3.1 line 311 listing references 'Code knowledge graph. 66 languages, 14 tools. Sub-ms structural queries.' which corresponds to the well-known codebase-memory-mcp project; if multiple candidate upstreams exist with that name, pick the one whose advertised tool surface most closely matches the master-plan claim — '14 tools' is a strong upstream-disambiguation signal; document the chosen upstream's repo URL and the disambiguation rationale in the TOML comment block); (b) pinned version (executor identifies the latest stable release tag or commit SHA as of 2026-05; do NOT use `main` / `latest` / `master` floating refs — the acceptance grep `! grep -q '"main"' plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml` enforces); (c) advertised tool list (executor runs `health_check` once during dev to discover, then bakes the assertion into the integration test); (d) transport mode (`stdio` per master-plan §13); (e) Phase 3 Week 9 G3 source identification verbatim ('G3 Knowledge source per master-plan §5.3 lines 463-475'); (f) revisit clause ('supersede via ADR if upstream cuts a v1.x with breaking tool-surface changes'). `[plugin]` table: `name = "codebase-memory"`, `version = "<pinned>"`, `description = "Code knowledge graph with sub-ms structural queries (G3 source per master-plan §5.3)."`. `[capabilities]` table: `provides = ["knowledge.symbol_lookup", "knowledge.relation_query"]` (executor refines these capability strings based on the upstream's actual tool surface; `knowledge.*` namespace is the convention going forward — the existing serena manifest does NOT use a namespaced `knowledge.*` prefix, so this is a NEW namespace; document the choice in the TOML comment block as 'establishing the `knowledge.*` capability namespace for G3 sources, paralleling `search.*` from probe and `navigate.cross-repo` from scip'). `languages = [...]` (executor picks the canonical codebase-memory-mcp-supported languages — at minimum rust, typescript, python, go, java; the master-plan claim '66 languages' is the upper bound, but the manifest list should reflect the actual languages exercised by the verify-script smoke, NOT a 66-element list). `[transport]` table: `type = "stdio"`, `command` and `args` set so the launched subprocess is a REAL MCP server speaking JSON-RPC over stdio. Likely shapes the executor researches: `npx -y <pkg>@<pinned>` OR `uvx --from <git-ref> <bin>` OR `pip install <pkg>==<pinned> && <bin>`. Pick ONE pinned variant. `[lifecycle]`: `hot_cold = true`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 10`. NO `main`-branch refs; pin to a tag or commit SHA.

2. plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml — NEW. Same shape. Leading TOML comment block citing: (a) upstream URL — Mem0 is the well-known agent-memory project at https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0 per master-plan §3.1 line 312 ('Agent memory. Three-tier (user/session/agent). Self-editing conflict resolution.'). The MCP-server entry point is upstream-published — executor researches the canonical invocation (likely `uvx --from <pinned> mem0-mcp-server` OR `npx -y @mem0/mcp-server@<pinned>` OR `npx -y mem0ai-mcp@<pinned>`); document the chosen invocation rationale. Pin to a specific release tag or commit SHA; do NOT use `main`/`latest`/`master`. (b) Advertised tool list (executor runs `health_check` once during dev to discover the canonical Mem0 MCP tool names — typical Mem0 surface includes tools like `add_memory`, `search_memory`, `get_memory`, `list_memories`, `delete_memory`; bake assertions on at least the three CRUD tools the master-plan-§3.1-line-312 description mandates: `store`, `retrieve`, `list`. Map the master-plan's CRUD vocabulary to whatever the upstream actually advertises — if upstream uses `add_memory`/`search_memory`/`list_memories`, document the mapping in the TOML comment). (c) Transport mode (`stdio`). (d) G3 source identification ('G3 Knowledge source per master-plan §5.3 lines 463-475; agent-memory complement to codebase-memory'). (e) Note on optional Python SDK — the master-plan listing says 'Python SDK + MCP server'; the SDK is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO (the manifest only declares the MCP transport). Python SDK installation lives in `scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh` as a hint, not a hard dependency. (f) Revisit clause. `[plugin]`: `name = "mem0"`, `version = "<pinned>"`, `description = "Three-tier agent memory (user/session/agent) with self-editing conflict resolution (G3 source per master-plan §5.3)."`. `[capabilities]`: `provides = ["knowledge.memory_crud", "knowledge.memory_search"]`, `languages = []` (mem0 is language-agnostic — agent memory is text-keyed, not code-language-keyed; document the empty list as a deliberate choice in the TOML comment). `[transport]`: same pinned-canonical-MCP-launch principle as codebase-memory. `[lifecycle]`: `hot_cold = true`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 10`.

3. scripts/devtools/install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh — NEW. Idempotent shell script: detect existing upstream binary on PATH (`command -v <bin>`), print version + exit 0 if present and version matches the manifest pin. Otherwise print the recommended install command (e.g. `npm install -g <pkg>@<pinned>` OR `uv tool install <pkg>==<pinned>` OR `pipx install <pkg>==<pinned>`) and exit 0 (do NOT auto-run the install — operators may choose how). Mark as executable (`chmod +x`). The installer follows the WO-0044 install-ast-grep / install-probe shape verbatim. Top-of-file comment cites the manifest path so operators can cross-reference.

4. scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh — NEW. Same idempotent pattern. Detect, print version, otherwise print install hint. Document the optional Python SDK install (`pip install mem0ai==<pinned>`) as a SEPARATE second hint after the MCP-server install hint — the master-plan listing distinguishes the two, but the WO-0069 manifest only declares the MCP transport.

5. crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs — NEW integration test file (separate from `plugin_manifests.rs` to keep the WO-0044 regression guard untouched and to scope the G3-specific opt-out env distinctly). Wrap tests in `mod g3_plugin_manifests { ... }` block so nextest reports them as `g3_plugin_manifests::codebase_memory_manifest_health_check` etc. — same wrapper pattern as `plugin_manifests.rs:36`. Two `#[tokio::test]` async tests: `codebase_memory_manifest_health_check` and `mem0_manifest_health_check`. Each: (a) loads the manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path(workspace_root.join("plugins/knowledge/<name>/plugin.toml"))` — discover the workspace root via `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` + `..` traversal (mirror `plugin_manifests.rs:52-60` `repo_root()` helper). (b) Manifest sanity assertions BEFORE paying the npx/uvx cost: `assert_eq!(manifest.plugin.name, "<name>")`, `assert_eq!(manifest.transport.kind, "stdio")`, `assert!(!manifest.capabilities.provides.is_empty())`. (c) Calls `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(&manifest, FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS).await.expect("health-check <name> MCP server")` with `const FIRST_RUN_TIMEOUT_MS: u64 = 90_000;` (mirror `plugin_manifests.rs:47`). (d) Asserts `health.name == "<name>"`, `health.status == HealthStatus::Ok`, `!health.tools.is_empty()`, and `health.tools.iter().any(|t| t == "<expected-tool-name>")` — executor pins the expected tool name based on what the upstream MCP server actually advertises (run health_check once during dev to discover, then bake the assertion). For F05: pick a tool whose name semantically matches `symbol_lookup` from the master-plan F05 description (likely `search_symbols`, `find_symbol`, or `get_symbol`; whatever upstream advertises). For F06: pick at least one of the three CRUD tools the master-plan F06 description mandates (`store`, `retrieve`, `list` — mapped to whatever upstream actually advertises like `add_memory`/`search_memory`/`list_memories`). NO mocks of `tokio::process::Command`. NO `#[ignore]`. Honour `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` opt-out (mirror `plugin_manifests.rs:62-67` `skip_via_env()` helper) AND a NEW `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E` opt-out specifically for these two tests (so an operator can run only the WO-0044 plugin_manifests suite without paying the cost of two additional 90-second cold-cache npx/uvx fetches). The G3-specific env is the canonical opt-out for both of these tests; document it inline. The verifier MUST NOT set EITHER opt-out. The test file's docstring (per the `plugin_manifests.rs:1-34` pattern) explicitly cites the no-mock invariant + which workspace fixture projects the smoke command exercises + the WO-0044 / DEC-0007 precedents.

6. scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh — REWRITE the stub (current stub is `echo TODO; exit 1`). Shape: (a) `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, (b) `set -euo pipefail`, (c) `IFS=$'\n\t'`, (d) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`, (e) checks for the upstream binary on PATH (e.g. `command -v <bin>` OR — for npx-launched plugins — `command -v npx`) and exits 1 with operator-actionable message + `scripts/devtools/install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh` reference if absent, (f) prints the binary version for the verifier log, (g) runs `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::codebase_memory_manifest_health_check 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0069-f05-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/wo-0069-f05-cargo.log` (alternation pattern matching both cargo-test and cargo-nextest, lesson from WO-0038/0039/0042/0043/0044), (h) runs a tool-level symbol-lookup smoke gated by `${UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}` (skip the smoke section if set) — invokes the plugin's symbol-lookup tool against `tests/fixtures/rust-project` (e.g. spawn the manifest's transport.command via a small `mcp_smoke` shell helper using `tools/call` with `arguments: {symbol: "evaluate"}` per the `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128 pub fn evaluate` fixture-anchor convention from WO-0044/0055 lessons; alternative: invoke the upstream's CLI subcommand if one exists for symbol lookup); asserts the response is valid JSON with at least one expected field (e.g. `results[]` non-empty OR `symbols[]` non-empty — executor picks based on actual upstream response shape). (i) On all-green prints `[OK] P3-W9-F05` and exits 0; on any failure prints `[FAIL] P3-W9-F05: <reason>` and exits 1. `chmod +x` so it's executable.

7. scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh — REWRITE the stub. Same shape as F05 but for Mem0 + agent-memory CRUD: (a-g) preamble, version print, install hint, frozen cargo-test, (h) tool-level CRUD smoke gated by `${UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}` — exercise three operations in sequence against an ephemeral tmpdir-rooted Mem0 store: STORE — `tools/call` with `arguments: {observation: "<text>", key: "wo-0069-smoke"}` (or whatever the upstream's `add_memory` shape is); RETRIEVE — `tools/call` with `arguments: {key: "wo-0069-smoke"}` and assert the returned text matches the stored text; LIST — `tools/call` with `arguments: {limit: 10}` and assert the returned list contains the stored entry. The executor MAY use a small shell helper or a one-off Python snippet using `mcp_smoke` to drive the JSON-RPC dialogue (mirror what WO-0044 P2-W6-F06 does for probe — same shape, just different tool names). The store MUST be ephemeral: use `mktemp -d` for the data dir and pass via env or args to the plugin so the test does not pollute a global Mem0 store. (i) On all-green prints `[OK] P3-W9-F06` and exits 0; on any failure prints `[FAIL] P3-W9-F06: <reason>` and exits 1. `chmod +x`.

8. The integration test file (g3_plugin_manifests.rs) MUST be additive only — `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044) MUST NOT be touched. Two distinct test files keep the WO-0044 regression guard isolated and let the G3-specific 90-second-cold-cache opt-out flag work cleanly without forcing the WO-0044 tests to pay the same cost (operators may run F05/F06 verify once and then set `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E=1` for subsequent fast cycles, while keeping `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` unset for the WO-0044 ast-grep + probe regression coverage).

9. Commit ladder — approximately 6 commits, each ≤50 LOC except the integration-test file (DEC-0005 module-coherence-commits applies): (1) `feat(plugins): add codebase-memory plugin manifest under plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/` (~30-50 LOC TOML + comment block); (2) `feat(plugins): add mem0 plugin manifest under plugins/knowledge/mem0/` (~30-50 LOC); (3) `chore(devtools): add install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh + install-mem0-mcp.sh helpers` (~40 LOC across two files); (4) `test(daemon): add g3_plugin_manifests integration suite covering codebase-memory + mem0 health-check` (~120-160 LOC; module-coherence carve-out for the single coherent test file per DEC-0005 + WO-0044 precedent); (5) `feat(verify): implement scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh end-to-end` (~70-100 LOC bash); (6) `feat(verify): implement scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh end-to-end` (~70-100 LOC bash). Per-commit Conventional Commits format with body trailers: `Phase: 3`, `Feature: P3-W9-F05` (or F06), `Work-order: WO-0069`, `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`.

10. Pre-flight word-ban grep (per WO-0061 line 686 + WO-0066 line 39 carry): NO literal `mock`, `fake`, `stub` substrings (case-insensitive) in the manifest TOML files OR the install scripts OR the verify scripts. The integration test file's `Test*` type names + `#[cfg(test)]` blocks are exempt per WO-0048 line 363; non-#[cfg(test)] sections are subject to the ban. Pre-commit grep: `grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml plugins/knowledge/mem0/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-codebase-memory-mcp.sh scripts/devtools/install-mem0-mcp.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh` MUST return empty. The g3_plugin_manifests.rs file is exempt under #[cfg(test)] per WO-0048 line 363.

11. Pre-baked mutation contract documented in WO `acceptance` block AND executor's `ready-for-review.md` Mutation-execution-log section — three mutations the verifier applies in-place via `Edit` (NOT `git stash`), takes md5sum snapshot before, restores via `git checkout --` after, confirms md5 match. M1 — codebase-memory transport.command poison: in `plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml`, replace the `[transport].command` field with `"/__ucil_test_nonexistent_codebase_memory_binary__"`. Expected: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g3_plugin_manifests g3_plugin_manifests::codebase_memory_manifest_health_check` fails with `PluginError::Spawn` (process spawn fails, health-check returns Err). Verifier restoration: `git checkout -- plugins/knowledge/codebase-memory/plugin.toml`. M2 — mem0 transport.command poison: same shape against the mem0 manifest. Expected: the mem0 test fails with PluginError::Spawn. Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`. M3 — expected-tool-name regression: in `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs`, change the `health.tools.iter().any(|t| t == "<expected-tool>")` assertion's literal string to `"__ucil_test_nonexistent_tool_name__"`. Expected: the test fails with the assertion's structured panic message naming the missing tool. Verifier restoration: `git checkout --`.

12. NO new daemon Cargo deps. NO new top-level workspace deps. The integration test imports only `ucil_daemon::{HealthStatus, PluginManager, PluginManifest}` (already public re-exports) and uses `tokio` (already a workspace dev-dep). If any helper crate is needed for the verify-script's MCP smoke driver (e.g. a shell-level `mcp_smoke` helper), it MUST be a stand-alone bash function in the verify script itself — DO NOT add a `crates/ucil-mcp-smoke` helper crate. The cleanest pattern is a single shell function that opens fd 3 (read) + fd 4 (write) on the spawned subprocess, sends a JSON-RPC line, reads a JSON-RPC line, parses with `python3 -c 'import json,sys; d = json.load(sys.stdin); ...'` (python3 is universally available; no extra dep). Mirror what scripts/verify/P2-W6-F05.sh / P2-W6-F06.sh do for ast-grep / probe.

13. Coverage gate posture — `crates/ucil-daemon` coverage MUST NOT regress below pre-WO baseline. Since this WO adds no new daemon source code (only a new tests/g3_plugin_manifests.rs integration test file), the daemon line-coverage percentage will move only marginally (the `health_check` code path is already covered by `plugin_manifests.rs` so the new tests are net-zero on coverage for src/). The coverage workaround standing protocol (now 25 WOs deep — WO-0058..WO-0069) carries: `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` reports `>= 80.0`; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh` `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-daemon` line is INFORMATIONAL-ONLY per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

14. Tracing posture — NONE. The integration tests exercise the existing `PluginManager::health_check` + `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` code paths whose tracing spans were established in WO-0042/0043. F05/F06 add no new traced surface; the integration test file emits no `tracing::*` calls of its own (mirror `plugin_manifests.rs` which has none). The verify scripts emit informational `echo` lines for operator readability, not structured logs.

15. WO-0068 lessons-learned `scope_in` carve-out for gate-side artefact commits: pre-emptively carve out `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` AND `ucil-build/escalations/**` from `forbidden_paths`. Phase-N gate-check + effectiveness-evaluator child agents commit refresh artefacts and `harness-config` escalations to the feat branch during gate execution. The current carve-out is the working template from WO-0067 scope_in #42 / WO-0068 scope_in #50. Critic / verifier may flag as a Warning but accept as benign gate-side artefact.

16. WO-0067/WO-0068 deferred re-export bundle is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO. Re-exports of `classify_query` (from `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` per WO-0067) and `parse_reason` (from `crates/ucil-core/src/ceqp.rs` per WO-0067) into `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` are deferred to the FIRST daemon-side WO that wires the classifier into orchestration (a future WO that touches `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` or `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs` to route incoming MCP queries through `classify_query` before group dispatch). WO-0069 is plugin-manifest-only and does NOT touch `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs`; carrying the deferred re-exports forward to a downstream WO is the correct treatment per WO-0067 lesson §'For planner' and WO-0068 lesson §'For planner'.

17. Upstream-API research checklist for the executor (prevent hallucination): (a) For codebase-memory: search npm + pypi + crates.io + GitHub for the canonical `codebase-memory-mcp` upstream; verify the package exists and ships a stdio MCP server entry-point. The master-plan §3.1 line 311 description ('Code knowledge graph. 66 languages, 14 tools. Sub-ms structural queries.') is a strong disambiguation signal — '14 tools' is the upstream's `tools/list` length and should be verifiable via `health_check` once the manifest is wired. (b) For mem0: the upstream is https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0 (well-known); the MCP server entry-point is documented in the upstream README — the executor researches the canonical 2026-05 invocation (likely `uvx --from <git-ref> mem0-mcp` or `npx -y @mem0/mcp-server@<version>`). The Mem0 Python SDK (`pip install mem0ai`) is the alternative non-MCP path documented in master-plan §3.1 line 312 — the WO declares the MCP transport only. (c) For BOTH plugins: pin to a specific tag or commit SHA. NO `main`, `latest`, `master`, `head`, `develop`, `dev`, `nightly`, or other moving refs. The pre-commit hook + acceptance grep `! grep -qE '"(main|latest|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/knowledge/<name>/plugin.toml` enforces. (d) Both plugins MAY require runtime API keys (OpenAI / Anthropic / etc.) for embedding-backed operations. The MCP `tools/list` round-trip MUST work without API keys (this is a strict prerequisite; if a plugin refuses to advertise tools without an API key, escalate via a proposed-decision before continuing). The tool-level smoke (verify-script step h) MAY require an API key — that section is gated by `UCIL_SKIP_KNOWLEDGE_PLUGIN_E2E=1` opt-out. The verifier MUST be able to run the integration test (which is health_check only) without any API keys present. Document any required API-key env-vars in the install-script comment block.

18. Phase-1 + Phase-2 gate regression check — `bash scripts/gate/phase-1.sh` MUST exit 0 post-merge AND `bash scripts/gate/phase-2.sh` MUST exit 0 post-merge (carry from WO-0058..WO-0068 standing protocol). The new manifests parse via existing `PluginManifest::from_path` (no schema changes), exercise existing `health_check` (no spawn-path changes), and add no new daemon source — no Phase-1 or Phase-2 invariant should regress.

19. Phase-3 gate regression check — `bash scripts/gate/phase-3.sh` exits non-zero is EXPECTED (Phase 3 is in progress, only F01-F04 pass currently). The relevant assertion is that `phase-3.sh`'s F05 + F06 sub-checks succeed AFTER this WO merges; verify by running `bash scripts/verify/P3-W9-F05.sh` and `bash scripts/verify/P3-W9-F06.sh` directly in the WO's verification report.

20. Verification-reports + effectiveness-evaluator out-of-scope re-runs — phase-N effectiveness-evaluator child agents MAY commit `ucil-build/verification-reports/effectiveness-phase-3.md` updates on the WO-0069 feat branch (carry from WO-0067 line 779 + WO-0068 carry observation). Critic / verifier may flag as a Warning but accept as benign gate-side artefact. Do NOT add `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` to `forbidden_paths` — that would block legitimate gate-side commits.
scope_out: NO changes to ANY Rust source under `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**`. The plugin manifest parser (WO-0042) and lifecycle/hot-reload/circuit-breaker code (WO-0042/0043) is FROZEN for this WO. Both new manifests exercise existing `PluginManifest`, `PluginManager::health_check`, `PluginManager::discover` surfaces unchanged.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml`. The integration test imports `ucil_daemon::{HealthStatus, PluginManager, PluginManifest}` (already public re-exports) and uses `tokio` (already a workspace dev-dep). If a new dev-dep is genuinely required, escalate via ADR before adding.

NO changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (WO-0044 P2-W6-F05/F06 regression guard — frozen). NO mock of `tokio::process::Command`, `Child`, `ChildStdio`, or any other process-spawning surface in the new integration tests. The whole point of F05/F06 is that real MCP-server subprocesses speak real JSON-RPC over stdio.
NO `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.
